steps.title: Identify the central finding
steps.content: The text defines microplastics, explains that they are found in ocean water, and then focuses on Keller's research: reef-building corals may store up to 20 million kilograms of microplastics each year in their skeletons and tissues.

steps.title: Separate background from main idea
steps.content: A shifts to how corals build reefs, B makes a vague claim about ecosystems, and C only repeats background information about microplastics. The main idea is the predicted large-scale storage of microplastics by corals. The answer is D.
